content: We can reframe intrinsic curiosity bonuses in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (CDRL) as a tool-based memory operation, where ICM and RND's prediction error signals act as dynamic, environment-responsive tools for pruning and curating an agent's long-term memory instead of just encouraging exploration. This creates a 'curiosity gatekeeping memory system that automatically prioritizes storing surprising, learnable new memories while discarding uninformative noise, aligning both the intrinsic reward objective and tool-based memory management goals.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
